verification.reason: Paper states anthocyanin levels were 'at concentrations comparable to the anthocyanin levels found in blackberries and blueberries.' The broader gene activation spectrum vs Lc/C1 is discussed in detail in the Discussion. The mouse lifespan data (182.2 ± 8.6 d vs 142.0 ± 8.7 d, P < 0.05) matches Figure 4 exactly: 'Standard diet: 142.0 ± 8.7 d' and 'Purple tomato: 182.2 ± 8.6 d average lifespan' with 'significantly longer average life span (182.2 days; P < 0.05).'

The paper states E8 is a 'fruit-specific' promoter, pigmentation 'extending to peel, pericarp and inner flesh' (Fig. 1c,d), and 'Stems and leaves showed no abnormal anthocyanin accumulation.' All three claims are explicitly stated.
